children.label: A
children.text: the identification of the foreign country with which U.S. Customs and Border Protection intends to enter into a preclearance agreement;

children.label: B
children.text: the location at which such preclearance operations will be conducted; and

children.label: C
children.text: the terms and conditions for U.S. Customs and Border Protection personnel operating at the location;
an assessment of the impact such preclearance operations will have on legitimate trade and travel, including potential impacts on passengers traveling to the United States;
an assessment of the impacts such preclearance operations will have on U.S. Customs and Border Protection domestic port of entry staffing;
country-specific information on the anticipated homeland security benefits associated with establishing such preclearance operations;
information on potential security vulnerabilities associated with commencing such preclearance operations and mitigation plans to address such potential security vulnerabilities;
a U.S. Customs and Border Protection staffing model for such preclearance operations and plans for how such positions would be filled; and
information on the anticipated costs over the 5 fiscal years after the agreement enters into force associated with commencing such preclearance operations.

children.heading: Further notification relating to preclearance operations established at airports
children.label: b Further notification relating to preclearance operations established at airports

children.text: an estimate of the date on which U.S. Customs and Border Protection intends to establish preclearance operations under such agreement, including any pending caveats that must be resolved before preclearance operations are approved;
the anticipated funding sources for preclearance operations under such agreement, and other funding sources considered;
a homeland security threat assessment for the country in which such preclearance operations are to be established;
information on potential economic, competitive, and job impacts on United States air carriers associated with establishing such preclearance operations;
details on information sharing mechanisms to ensure that U.S. Customs and Border Protection has current information to prevent terrorist and criminal travel; and
other factors that the Secretary determines to be necessary for Congress to comprehensively assess the appropriateness of commencing such preclearance operations.

children.heading: Certifications relating to preclearance operations established at airports
children.label: c Certifications relating to preclearance operations established at airports

children.text: a certification that preclearance operations under such preclearance agreement, after considering alternative options, would provide homeland security benefits to the United States through the most effective means possible;

children.label: A
children.text: at least one United States passenger carrier operates at such airport; and

children.label: B
children.text: any United States passenger carriers operating at such airport and desiring to participate in preclearance operations are provided access that is comparable to that of any non-United States passenger carrier operating at that airport;
a certification that the establishment of preclearance operations in such foreign country will not significantly increase customs processing times at United States airports;

a certification that representatives from U.S. Customs and Border Protection consulted with stakeholders, including providers of commercial air service in the United States, employees of such providers, security experts, and such other parties as the Secretary determines to be appropriate; and
a report detailing the basis for the certifications referred to in paragraphs (1) through (4).
a copy of the agreement, as amended; and
the justification for such amendment.

children.label: A
children.text: the number of U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers, by port, assigned from domestic ports of entry to preclearance operations; and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of the positions at domestic ports of entry vacated by U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers described in subparagraph (A) that have been filled by other hired, trained, and equipped U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: 2 Submission
children.text: If the Commissioner has not filled the positions of U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers that were reassigned to preclearance operations and determines that U.S. Customs and Border Protection processing times at domestic ports of entry from which U.S. Customs and Border Protection officers were reassigned to preclearance operations have significantly increased, the Commissioner, not later than 60 days after making such a determination, shall submit to the appropriate congressional committees an implementation plan for reducing processing times at the domestic ports of entry with such increased processing times.

children.heading: Suspension
children.label: 3 Suspension
children.text: If the Commissioner does not submit the implementation plan described in paragraph (2) to the appropriate congressional committees before the deadline set forth in such paragraph, the Commissioner may not commence preclearance operations at an additional port of entry in any country until such implementation plan is submitted.

children.heading: Classified report
children.label: f Classified report
children.text: The report required under subsection (c)(5) may be submitted in classified form if the Secretary determines that such form is appropriate.
